headline: Berkshire is extra bullish on Delta, Alphabet as CEO adjusts portfolio
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (BRK-A, BRK-B) has adjusted its equity portfolio under new CEO Greg Abel, including a new $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines (DAL) while tripling the firm's stake in Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL). The Glenview Trust Company CIO Bill Stone takes a closer look at Berkshire's revamped equity holdings and which stocks it has dumped under Abel. After stepping down from the chief executive role at the beginning of 2026, Warren Buffett remains as Berkshire's executive chairman.

The Glenview Trust Company CIO Bill Stone takes a closer look at Berkshire's revamped equity holdings and which stocks it has dumped under Abel. After stepping down from the chief executive role at the beginning of 2026, Warren Buffett remains as Berkshire's executive chairman.

headline: Visa vs Mastercard. The Numbers Just Told Us Which Network Is Pulling Away
summary: Visa (NYSE:V) and Mastercard (NYSE:MA) just closed the books on calendar Q1 2026, and both networks beat expectations while pulling toward different strategic frontiers. Visa is selling itself as a “payments hyperscaler”. Mastercard is leaning into agentic commerce and stablecoins. The same swipe economy, two very different bets on what comes next. headline: Digital Payment Today - Licensing Evolves Amidst Tech-Driven Finance Shift
summary: The Neves Licensing Authority has identified a growing global demand for specialized licensing frameworks in the digital finance sector, driven by the shift towards technology-driven financial models and cross-border fintech activities. As financial technology operators increasingly depend on sophisticated systems like remote onboarding and cloud-based infrastructure, there's a rising need for modern licensing environments that support operational scalability and governance transparency. headline: Visa and Jason Sudeikis Turn the Simplest Goal in Football Into the Biggest Fan Moments at the FIFA World Cup 2026™
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, May 18, 2026--At the FIFA World Cup 2026™, everything can change in a split second. A quick pass. A simple finish. A tap in goal. This summer, Visa (NYSE: V), the Worldwide Payment Technology Partner of the FIFA World Cup 2026™, is turning those fleeting moments into something much bigger—launching Tap In, a bold new global campaign based on the belief that everything is a tap in with Visa.

headline: One Lane Closed. The Whole Rate Picture Changed
summary: One shipping lane controls roughly 20 percent of the world's oil supply. Right now, it's closed. And in the space of five trading days, that single fact has moved oil to $105 a barrel, pushed Treasury yields to their highest point in roughly a year, and resurrected a rate hike scenario that nobody was pricing a week ago. Welcome to Monday.

headline: Xi seems confident he can double-down on his relationship with Trump, says The Asia Group
summary: Kurt Tong, managing partner of The Asia Group and former Consul General of the U.S. to Hong Kong and Macau, says while the business deals announced during the Trump-Xi summit were significant, what's more important was the politics of President Trump signaling to the business constituency that there is "forward movement" in the relationship between the two countries.
